I treated the 32 prior turns as a proposal log, not as proof. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format: sigma,tau are distinguished, the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 is implicit, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are represented modulo M=85667662080.The prior log‚Äôs only still-live candidate is the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äď32; the earlier scalar, plain ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 candidates are refuted there by explicit finite-tame or index-two subgroup tests.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2 and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).

For clarity: the representative of ő∑=2ŌÄ used here is not the literal residue of 2ŌÄ modulo the 28 -part of M. Its 2-primary coordinate is chosen harmlessly because it is used only as an exponent of the tame generator ŌĄ, whose finite quotients have odd order. On the odd part of M, the stated value has the required congruences.The external literature picture is consistent with the prior log‚Äôs framing: Neukirch‚Äôs report describes the Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg approach as an explicit determination of local absolute Galois groups via tame subextensions and DemuŇ°kin-formation data, while Jarden‚ÄďShusterman record finite presentability/generator-relation counts for local absolute Galois groups.Numdam+1 Labute‚Äôs dyadic DemuŇ°kin classification is exactly the invariant package being tested in the prior turns: rank, cup product, and the 2-adic orientation/root-of-unity invariant.Numdam My main audit finding this turn is that the last several turns still have a dangerous displayed fraction backwards in the sign-cover calculation. The conclusion they wanted is salvageable, but the written formula is not.Let V=kerőļ be the sign double cover of a finite-tame pro-2 kernel. Let t be the deck involution, and work on a nontrivial tame-inertia character block where ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. For a norm wordNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the relevant Fox coefficient is the completed geometric sumDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

The correct identity isDa=1‚ąítőĪa 1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,because ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode), hence (tőĪa)ŌÄ=t. The reverse fraction (1‚ąítőĪa)/(1‚ąít), which appears in the latest prior text, is not a valid harmless rewrite: 1‚ąít is not a unit in the 2-adic sign-cover group algebra.With the corrected expression, the desired unit-pivot conclusion still holds. Since őĪaÓÄ†=1, the element 1‚ąítőĪa is a unit modulo the radical, while 1‚ąít lies in the radical. Thus Da is radical, and the coefficient contributed byx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. Therefore nontrivial odd tame-inertia character blocks contribute no 2-primary Smith torsion. This repairs, rather than invalidates, the prior sign-kernel argument.The other recurring direction issue is the trivial tame-inertia block. LetA=F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1),s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf),with f=2a m, m odd, so s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). The mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

When f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The legitimate elimination is therefore

Y=Q(T)X+YňČ,Q(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,not its reciprocal. With this Q, the reduced multiplier is h(T)=1+Q(T)+Q(T‚ąí1).

For a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1.

So h(ő∂)=0 would imply ő∂2s+1=1. But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), hence gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus the trivial block remains nonsingular. This agrees with the prior conclusion, but the quotient direction matters for any proof that is meant to be checkable.Using these corrected formulas, I rederive the uniform abelianization check for the finite-tame pro-2 kernels. ForőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,e¬†odd,¬†e‚ą£2f‚ąí1,let Ue,f be the pro-2 kernel induced by the candidate. PutőĽ=e‚ąí1j=0‚ąĎe‚ąí1ŌĄňČj‚ąąZ2[őĒe,f],the idempotent for the tame-inertia-invariant part, and let S=ŌÉf be the marked unramified pro-2 generator. Abelianizing the orbit of the candidate relation gives(ŌÉňČ‚ąís+őĽ)X+(őĽŌÉňČ‚ąí1)Y=0.

On the (1‚ąíőĽ)-part, this isŌÉňČ‚ąís X‚ąíY=0,so it contributes a free Z2 -module of rank (e‚ąí1)f and no torsion.

On the őĽ-part, the relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(T‚ąí1)Y=0overZ2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1).The T‚ąí1 incidence block supplies f‚ąí1 unit Smith pivots. Summing the cycle leaves the single relation2i=0‚ąĎf‚ąí1Xi=0.

So the invariant block contributes Z2f‚äēC2, and S contributes one additional free generator. HenceUe,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2.This is the expected local-class-field-theoretic shape before adjoining i: ef+1 free pro-2 directions and only őľ2 -torsion.

For the sign kernel Ve,f=kerőļ, the same corrected nontrivial-character calculation implies all nontrivial tame-inertia blocks have unit pivots. The only torsion comes from the ŌĄ-invariant unramified block. There the prior Smith calculation gives the nonunit factor 2(fq+s+1),q=f1‚ąís,so for the candidate this equals 4. Thus the repaired uniform conclusion isVe,fab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4.This is strong evidence for the candidate, but it is still not a proof of the full absolute Galois presentation. The late prior turns are right about the hard remaining issue: abstract DemuŇ°kin type, abelianization, cup product, sign kernel, and cyclotomic branch behavior do not force the exact filtered (ŌÉ,ŌĄ)-operator action. A final proof still needs a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem identifying the candidate‚Äôs exact action, includingŌÉŌÄ=ŌÉsS (1‚ąís)/f in finite unramified quotients and the dynamic tame correction ŌĄ‚ąí2ŌÄ, with the arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action over every finite tame subextension.

ESTABLISHED: The live candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected the sign-cover Fox coefficient toDa=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,not the reversed fraction appearing in the latest prior turns. With that correction, the nontrivial tame-inertia blocks still have unit Smith pivots. I also fixed the trivial block elimination to

Q(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,and rederivedUe,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4.

GAP: This is not yet a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing step is the dyadic formation/rigidity theorem: the exact filtered operator action selected by the relation must be identified with the arithmetic action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Stop treating abelian and cyclotomic Smith tests as independent evidence. Formulate the exact marked operator package (Ue,f,Ōá,S,ŌÉŌÄ,ŌĄ‚ąí2ŌÄ), then compare it with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic in the first mixed tame cases (e,f)=(3,2) and (5,4) through U/U5, where filtered-action ambiguities are visible.[BREAKTHROUGH]
